description:
Today every fn unconditionally gets a SpecId.0 == FnId.0 any-key spec keyed on `vec![Descr::any(); arity]`, alongside any narrow specs from observed callsites. The any-key is the uniform dispatch target for indirect callers: closures, spawn, receive. For fns that are never indirectly dispatched (the common case — see fixtures/add1.fz where main only direct-calls add1), the any-key is dead code in AOT output.

Flip the default from emit-then-DCE to emit-on-demand: register the any-key spec iff the fn appears in any non-call position in the IR.

## Detection rule

After ir_lower, walk module.fns building `indirectly_used: HashSet<FnId>` from these IR shapes:

- FnRef / fn-as-value (assignment, arg passing)
- MakeClosure capturing this fn
- Spawn(fn_id, ...)
- Receive handler installation

Then in the typer's spec-registration logic:

```
for each fn:
    register narrow specs from observed callsites   (existing .29 behavior)
    if fn_id ∈ indirectly_used:
        register any-key spec                       (new — conditional)
```

## Effect on add1.fz

Today: two symbols emitted (`add1` any-key, `add1_s1` narrow i64→i64).
After: one symbol emitted (`add1` narrow i64→i64). Any-key never exists.

## Effect on fns used both directly and indirectly

A fn that's both direct-called and closure-captured still gets both: narrow specs for the direct callsites plus the any-key for indirect dispatch. Rule is additive, not exclusive.

## Scope

- src/ir_lower.rs or src/ir_typer.rs: indirect-use detection pass (one IR walk, no fixpoint)
- src/ir_typer.rs: condition any-key registration on the result
- src/ir_codegen.rs: verify no consumer assumes any-key always exists; conservative-fallback paths (line ~759) need to handle absence gracefully or be reachable only when fn ∈ indirectly_used
- Update stale comment at src/ir_typer.rs:62 (the .29.1 doc still says "specs populated but not yet consumed" — false since .29.2 / .29.7 landed)

## Tests

- fixtures/add1.fz emits exactly one symbol; verify via fz dump --emit clif (fz-ul4.23.3)
- A fn passed as a closure value emits both narrow + any-key
- A fn used as Spawn target gets an any-key
- A purely direct-called fn has no any-key in the SpecRegistry
- Existing closure/spawn/receive tests still pass

## Out of scope

- Cross-module indirect-use analysis (today this is single-module; if cross-module FnRef becomes a thing, revisit)
- Eliminating narrow specs that aren't reached (separate DCE concern)
title: On-demand any-key spec registration
